rulings.Case Title: DANIEL JACOB, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. LAIT BALCHANDANI, AS TRUSTEE OF THE RAHUL BHIDE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly allege all elements of the cause of action for quiet title; attach the deed which forms the basis of Plaintiff's claim (FAC 3:28-4:1); properly name the estate as the defendant if Plaintiff seeks a declaration against the estate's claims of ownership. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to participate in the mandatory meet and confer process. No further challenges to pleadings will be heard by the Court absent Plaintiff's full participation in the meet and confer process. If Plaintiff fails to participate, Defendant shall file declarations regarding extension until such time that Plaintiff complies with its statutory obligations. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MENGZHEN SUN ET AL VS. KOMENDI KOSASIH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right Of Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment Against Defendant Allan Young
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT ALLAN YOUNG is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show that this action contains a claim for money based on an express or implied contract where the total amount of such claim is a fixed or "readily ascertainable." Plaintiff may re-file their application including the "fixed" or "ascertained" amount of the claim for money. Reference to "great financial losses" (Complaint, paragraph 49) "more than $500," (Points and Authorities 10:1) is insufficient. Compare to Greenebaum v. Smith (1921) 51 Cal. App. 692; "plaintiff accordingly fixed his damage at the sum of twenty-eight thousand dollars." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PASCUAL AJANEL RAMOS RAMOS ET AL VS. CARMEN A. SOLIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is GRANTED. Defendants are ordered to not interfere with Plaintiff's possession and use of the premises including kitchen and bathroom facilities; all utilities must be functioning. A compliance hearing is set for October 19, 2022. By no later than October 13, 2022 each defendant to file a declaration attesting to non-interference with tenant's possession and established access to all amenities. By no later than October 17, 2022 tenants to post rent for 1 month (October 17 to November 16, 2022). Payment of future rent will be discussed at the compliance hearing. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HOMERISESF VS. DANA BETINIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. This action was filed on November 15, 2021 and Defendant appeared on December 1, 2021; trial set for October 31, 2022. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that this motion for judgment on the pleadings in a case that has been pending for 11 months is properly set for hearing in a law and motion department on a 4-day notice (2 court days for e-service). Kortmeyer v. California Ins. Guar. Ass'n (1992) 9 Cal. App. 4th 1285 and Lortz v. Connell (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 286 are procedurally distinguishable. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NORMANDY APARTMENTS VS. ENOE MARTINEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion, And Motion To Deem Defendant'S Admissions Admitted, To Compel Other Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents, To Compel Deposition Of Defendant And For Sanctions Against Defendant Enoe Martinez
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DEEM DEFENDANT'S ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, TO COMPEL OTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT ENOE MARTINEZ is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall appear for deposition on a mutually agreeable date between October 17 and October 21, 2022. If parties cannot agree on a date deposition shall proceed on October 20, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. Plaintiff to provide a 3-day personal, e-mail (if agreed) or overnight mail notice. Sanctions granted in the amount of $960. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. MICHAEL HENDERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Michael Henderson Notice Of Motion To Withdraw Or Amend Admissions That Were Deemed Admitted For Failure To Respond; Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Ryan D. Murphy In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT MICHAEL HENDERSON'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO WITHDRAW OR AMEND ADMISSIONS THAT WERE DEEMED ADMITTED FOR FAILURE TO RESPOND is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry his burden and show that admissions were the result of "mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect." Per Defendant's motion, defendant obtained help from EDC with filing his answer on August 19, 2022; defendant was served with discovery on August 26, 2022 and did not seek help from EDC until September 19, 2022, 3-days after being served with the motion to compel. At the time of the order deeming admissions admitted request for admissions was outstanding for over 3 weeks. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
